response.justification: Candidate has directly relevant geospatial/GIS experience (GISCollective, Green Map) and exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Sociomantic, millions of req/sec), plus systems-language background in DLang which transfers reasonably well to modern C++. However, the C/C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and there is no Rust experience. Most recent work is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack rather than systems-level pipeline engineering. Worth a screening call to assess current C++ depth and appetite to return to systems programming.
strengths: Founded and operates a GIS/geospatial data platform with real users
Distributed, low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, trunk-based development
concerns: Modern C++ experience is shallow and dated (~6 months, 2014-2015)
No Rust experience listed as an alternative path
Recent five years are TypeScript/Node.js product work, not pipeline/systems engineering
